ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch, for and with petitioners Gary L. Bradley and Randolph Bradley (Successor Co-Trustees)

The Court requested an update regarding the comingling of funds. Ms. Rasch stated that that issue was taken care of, as a separate account was created and the funds deposited therein, with the receipt for the deposit of the funds having been filed on February 17, 2015. The Court inquired if there were bank statements showing the name on the account into which the funds were deposited. Mrs. Rasch responded that the bank statements were filed with the court in April of 2015. The Court stated that the Probate Examiner had a further issue with the accounting, as it sought reimbursement for travel expenses for tolls, mileage and travel time which were not allowable. Ms. Rasch replied that she did not have examiner's notes to advise her of what expenses were challenged, but that the Co-Trustees had agreed not to seek payment of any trustees fees during the Catherine's lifetime, and only sought repayment of appropriate expenses. The Court stated that either the Court could make its best estimate of deductions for the inappropriate travel expenses, or counsel for the Co-Trustees could provide the Court with her own proposal for deductions to be made. Ms. Rasch responded that she would provide the Court with a supplemental declaration, at which time the matter would be submitted. The Court stated that he was granting approval of the accounting subject to deduction of travel costs, in an amount to be determined following receipt of the supplemental submission from Ms. Rasch. The Court set a status hearing in the matter for February 8, 2017, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Mr. Feder stated that there were a lot of unnecessary fees incurred in this case, that the money and property were in the the Trust, that the Conservator had concerns that the Trustees were not acting in the Conservatee's best interest, that he did not object to Ms. Orly's work that there was no benefit to my client, and that his client should not have to pay for it. Mr. Siracusa stated that there was no concept for extraordinary fees when following the one percent guideline, that there is a severe liquidity issue, that there is approxiamately $8,000.00 in checking, $47,000.00 in the annuity, and the rest is all real property. In addition, he stated that if fees were awarded they could not be paid until the Solano property was sold. Ms. Orly stated that they would wait for the money; this was agreed that it was in everyone's best interest. Mr. Feder stated that his client was ok with the conservatorship which was needed long ago. The matter was taken under submission.
